description: Add the root Go module and internal/checks README validator that enforces Phase 1-3 structure and entry rules (sections, Contents alignment, entry format, punctuation, marketing phrases, duplicates, alphabetization, URL hygiene) with hard failures and scope/tracking warnings, table-tested behavior, and a passing go run ./internal/checks against the current empty-section README—without GitHub Actions, templates, Makefile, or bulk content.

context.customerAsk: Phase 4 automation: add the root Go module and custom README checker requested in docs/internal/customer-ask.md. Implement go.mod, go.sum if needed, and internal/checks with focused code split across main.go, readme.go, links.go, sections.go, alphabetical.go, and duplicates.go or an equivalent small Go package structure that preserves those responsibilities. The checker must validate that README exists; required sections exist; Contents links match required headings; README entries use - [Name](URL) - Description.; descriptions end with a period; duplicate URLs and duplicate resource names are rejected; entries are alphabetized within sections; bare URLs are rejected; tracking-heavy or suspicious URLs are reported; marketing phrases such as revolutionary, game-changing, best, ultimate, and cutting-edge are rejected unless part of a resource title; and scope keywords are warnings rather than hard failures. Keep empty starter sections allowed until the content phase fills them. Include deterministic Go tests or table tests for the checker behavior using in-memory or fixture markdown. Do not add GitHub Actions, issue templates, or bulk README resource entries in this item. Acceptance: go test ./... passes, go run ./internal/checks passes against the current README, failures explain the specific resource-entry rule, and existing Phase 1-3 docs remain aligned.
context.problem: Phases 1-3 established README structure, governance, and manual review policy, but there is no executable enforcement of CONTRIBUTING and review-policy rules. Contributors and maintainers cannot run a deterministic local check for formatting, duplicates, alphabetization, promotional language, or scope signals before Phase 5 CI wiring.
context.solution: Introduce a root Go module (github.com/portpowered/awesome-agent-factories) and an internal/checks command that reads README.md, applies focused validations aligned with Phase 1-3 policy, prints actionable hard failures and non-blocking warnings, exits non-zero only on hard failures, and is covered by table-driven Go tests—allowing empty resource sections until Phase 7 content seeding.

acceptanceCriteria: Root go.mod (and go.sum if dependencies are used) exist with module path github.com/portpowered/awesome-agent-factories.
internal/checks is implemented across main.go, readme.go, links.go, sections.go, alphabetical.go, and duplicates.go preserving the specified responsibilities.
go test ./... passes with deterministic table tests for hard-failure and warning behaviors.
go run ./internal/checks exits 0 against the current repository README.md with empty resource sections allowed.
Hard failures identify the specific resource-entry rule, section, and entry context; scope and tracking findings are warnings only.
No GitHub Actions, issue templates, Makefile, bulk README entries, or unrelated refactors are added.
Quality gate: go build ./internal/checks succeeds; go test ./... passes; checker rules remain aligned with CONTRIBUTING.md, docs/review-policy.md, and README.md Phase 1 structure.

userStories.title: Bootstrap Go module and checks package layout
userStories.description: As a maintainer introducing Phase 4 automation, I want a root Go module and an internal/checks package skeleton so later validation stories have a stable build and ownership boundary.
acceptanceCriteria: go.mod exists at repository root with module path github.com/portpowered/awesome-agent-factories and a supported Go toolchain version
Package internal/checks exists with main.go, readme.go, links.go, sections.go, alphabetical.go, and duplicates.go preserving those responsibilities
go build ./internal/checks succeeds from repository root
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Load README and parse document structure
userStories.description: As a contributor running the checker locally, I want a clear failure when README.md is missing and reliable parsing of headings and sections so downstream rules operate on structured content.
acceptanceCriteria: Running the checker when README.md is absent exits non-zero with a message stating the README is missing
The checker reads README.md from the repository root and identifies ## section headings and their body content
Table tests use in-memory markdown fixtures to verify missing-README failure and basic section extraction
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Validate required sections and Contents-heading alignment
userStories.description: As a maintainer guarding list structure, I want required README sections and Contents anchor links to match headings so the Phase 1 skeleton cannot drift silently.
acceptanceCriteria: Hard failure when any required resource section heading is missing among the ten Phase 1 categories
Hard failure when ## Contents is missing or a Contents anchor does not match an existing ## heading for required resource sections
Empty resource sections with zero resource entries pass without error
Table tests cover missing section, missing Contents entry, and valid empty-section fixture
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Enforce resource entry list-item format
userStories.description: As a contributor adding a resource, I want invalid list-item shapes rejected with a specific format error so I can fix entries to match - [Name](URL) - Description.
acceptanceCriteria: Each resource entry must match - [Name](URL) - Description with a single markdown link and hyphen-separated description
Hard failure for malformed lines in resource sections with a message identifying section, approximate line, and expected format rule
Non-entry lines such as section intro paragraphs do not false-positive as entries
Table tests cover valid entry and multiple invalid shapes
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Enforce description punctuation and banned marketing phrases
userStories.description: As a maintainer enforcing encyclopedic tone, I want descriptions to end with a period and promotional phrases rejected unless they appear in the official resource title link text.
acceptanceCriteria: Hard failure when a resource description does not end with a period
Hard failure when description text contains banned phrases revolutionary, game-changing, best, ultimate, or cutting-edge case-insensitively unless only present in link text
Failure messages cite the entry and specific rule for missing period versus banned phrase
Table tests cover period violation, phrase in description, and phrase allowed in title only
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Reject duplicate URLs and duplicate resource names
userStories.description: As a maintainer preventing duplicate submissions, I want duplicate URLs and duplicate link-text names rejected anywhere in README resource entries.
acceptanceCriteria: Hard failure when the same normalized URL appears on more than one resource entry
Hard failure when the same resource name link text appears on more than one entry using case-insensitive comparison
Failure messages identify both colliding entries with section and name or URL
Table tests cover duplicate URL, duplicate name, and distinct entries
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Enforce alphabetical order within each resource section
userStories.description: As a contributor inserting a resource, I want alphabetization violations reported per section so I can place entries in the correct order by link text.
acceptanceCriteria: Hard failure when entries in a resource section are not sorted alphabetically by link text case-insensitively
Failure message names the section and out-of-order adjacent entries
Sections with zero or one entry pass alphabetization check
Table tests cover in-order, out-of-order, and single-entry sections
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Reject bare URLs and warn on tracking-heavy or suspicious links
userStories.description: As a maintainer protecting link quality, I want bare URLs rejected and tracking-heavy or suspicious URLs surfaced as warnings without failing an otherwise valid README.
acceptanceCriteria: Hard failure for bare http or https URLs in resource sections not inside markdown link syntax
Warning when a resource URL contains common tracking or suspicious patterns such as utm_, fbclid, gclid, or mc_eid
Warnings print with entry context and do not cause non-zero exit when no hard failures exist
Table tests cover bare URL failure, tracking URL warning, and clean canonical URL
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Emit scope-keyword warnings without failing valid structure
userStories.description: As a maintainer nudging agent-factory relevance, I want scope-related keyword gaps reported as warnings so contributors strengthen descriptions without blocking empty starter sections.
acceptanceCriteria: Warning when a resource description lacks any positive scope keyword from the CONTRIBUTING agent-factory angle set
Scope keyword findings are warnings only and never cause non-zero exit by themselves
Empty sections with no entries produce no scope warnings
Table tests cover description with scope keyword, without scope keyword, and empty section
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: CLI integration passes current README with aligned policy docs
userStories.description: As a factory operator completing Phase 4 core work, I want go run ./internal/checks to run all checks against the real README with clear aggregated output so the repository is ready for Makefile and CI wiring.
acceptanceCriteria: go run ./internal/checks from repository root runs all structure, format, duplicate, alphabetization, URL, marketing, and scope checks
Exit code 0 when current README.md has no hard failures with empty resource sections allowed
Exit code non-zero when any hard failure exists with stderr listing each failure and specific resource-entry rule
go test ./... passes for the full internal/checks package
Phase 1-3 docs are not contradicted and no doc claims GitHub Actions or CI already enforce checks
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
